summary: Implemented DiagnosisModal Bubble Tea model for the anomaly diagnosis overlay. Supports four states (No Anomalies, Has Anomalies, Evidence Selected, Error), double-line border (╔╗╚╝) for modal distinction, evidence blocks with icon + type tag (🟡 [slow] / 🔴 [unauthorized]), tool name + duration + line number, call chain context joined with →, thinking fragments truncated to 200 chars in bright cyan, reverse video highlight for selected evidence, and keyboard navigation (j/k navigate, Enter jump-back, Esc/q close). Jump-back emits JumpBackMsg with LineNum for the call tree to auto-expand parent Turn and scroll to target. Added JumpBackMsg type, DiagnosisState enum, and i18n keys (diagnosis.no_anomalies) for zh/en locales. Golden file tests for anomaly list rendering.
keyDecisions: Show() collects anomalies and thinkings from all turns in the session rather than requiring pre-computed data
Used map[int]string for thinkings (lineNum -> content) to efficiently look up thinking by anomaly LineNum
JumpBackMsg includes LineNum for call tree to find and scroll to target node
DiagnosisModal uses value receiver for immutable methods (SetError, SetSize) matching project pattern
Test functions prefixed with Diag to avoid name collisions with existing tests in same package
Thinking truncation uses len() on string (>200) consistent with detail.go pattern
Modal centered using lipgloss.Place with 80%x60% dimensions as specified in UI design

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: internal/model/diagnosis.go implements DiagnosisModal with Init(), Update(msg tea.Msg), View() string
States: No Anomalies, Has Anomalies, Evidence Selected, Error
Evidence blocks: icon + type tag (🟡 [slow] / 🔴 [unauthorized]), tool + duration + line, call chain, thinking
Thinking truncated to 200 chars in bright cyan
Keyboard: j/k navigate, Enter jump to call tree node (auto-expand parent Turn), Esc/q close
Jump-back scrolls call tree to target node and highlights it
Golden file tests for anomaly list rendering

notes: JumpBackMsg.TurnIdx field is included for future auto-expand support by the call tree model. The call tree's handleDiagnosis() emits DiagnosisRequestMsg which the app model will route to show the diagnosis modal.
